details: Paper organic_synthesis/fernandes_2018_pyridazine_suzuki.pdf line 1185 contains verbatim 'Yellow solid (14%). Mp: 207-210 ◦ C.' for compound 3b. DOI, compound name, value, data_type all match.

Quote 'Table 1: fenclofenac Melting point (deg C) 112' is a constructed paraphrase. The actual paper (2009_Yalkowsky_An_interesting_relationship_between_drug.pdf line 419) row reads 'Fenclofenac 297 112 g 1.00 c 400 c −4.55 e 4.71' — the constructed quote string is not present verbatim in the paper. Value 112 °C is correct.

Quote 'Table I. 1,2-diiodobenzene MP 27 BP 286' is a constructed paraphrase not present verbatim. The 1990 Yalkowsky paper (OCR) row reads '1,2-Diiodobenzene 27 286' with column headers elsewhere. Value 27 is correct.

Quote 'Table 1: nicardipine Melting point (deg C) 137' is a constructed paraphrase. Paper line 546 reads 'Nicardipine 466 137 g 1.00 b 25 b(avg) −5.33 e 5.51' — header concatenation absent. Value 137 is correct.

Paper 176_PMC11206253 article.pdf (laid-out, around line 2517-2522) contains verbatim 'Recrystallization of the product from hot ethanol afforded shiny yellow microcrystals (35.4% yield) with mp 146–147 ◦ C' for compound 8a. DOI/PMC, name, value, data_type all match.
Quote 'Table 1: nisoldipine Melting point (deg C) 152' is a constructed paraphrase. Paper line 567 reads 'Nisoldipine 388 152 j 0.95 b, c(avg) 15 b, c(avg) −5.63 h 4.86'. Value 152 is correct.

Quote 'm.p. 260-262 (Table 1, N-benzoyl-2-(4-nitrophenyl)-2-cyano-6,7-dihydro-9,...)' is a fabricated description, not verbatim text. Paper 054_PMC6146489 Table 1 row reads '13c brown 79 260-262 C29H22N4O5 68.77 4.35 11.07 -'. Value 260-262 matches; compound full name appears elsewhere as 'N-benzoylimino derivatives 13b,c' (line 262).

Compound name in row 'Methyl 4-((E)-benzylidene-1-phenyl-2',5'-dioxo) spiro compound' does not match paper's name '(E)-Methyl 4-((2',5'-dioxo-1'-phenylspiro[isochroman-3,3'-pyrrolidin]-4'-ylidene)methyl)benzoate' (organic_synthesis/yanovich_2024_spiroheterocycles_rh_catalysis.pdf line 803). Quote 'mp 189.2–189.9 °C' is verbatim at line 805 and value matches, but compound name is materially wrong (missing isochroman-pyrrolidin core).

Quote 'Table 2: 1,4-dioxane exp. mp 11 deg C' is a paraphrase. Paper (the file named 2009_Dearden_... is actually 2003 paper, DOI 10.1897/01-363) Table 2 line 359 reads '1,4-Dioxane 101.0 102.8 98.4 102.9 60.0 99.8 102.8 3.707 ... 11.8 263.6 234.5 274.0'. Value ~12 (rounded to 11) is approximately correct (exp mp column shows 11.8) but quote not verbatim.

Paper 014_PMC12943640 article.pdf lines 571-573 contains the quote substantively verbatim (with NFC/whitespace normalization): 'C5 H13 NO6 P2 : 244.0140, found 244.0138. N-(Piperidylamino)methylenebisphosphonic acid (27) was obtained with a 61% yield; mp. 241–244 ◦ C; 31 P NMR (242.93 MHz, D2 O + NaOD)'. Compound, value 241-244, data_type all match. DOI ph19020256 matches paper (note: row source field says vol 18 2025 but paper is vol 19 2026 — source URL/DOI agree with paper).

Paper organic_synthesis/yanovich_2024_spiroheterocycles_rh_catalysis.pdf line 579 names compound 2f as '(E)-7-Benzyl-9-benzylidene-4-methyl-1-oxa-7-azaspiro[4.4]non-3-ene-2,6,8-trione', and line 582 gives 'mp 167.2-168.0 °C' (using a Unicode dash). Compound name matches, value matches, data_type measured matches.

Quote 'II 340.34 C 17 H 16 N 4 O 4 60.00 (60.11) 4.74 (4.62) 16.46 (16.24) 112–114 Yellowish white' does not appear in paper 073_PMC13085778 article.pdf — no Table 1 with this content is present in the extracted text (only references to Table 1 for elemental analysis exist). The original row notes already flag this. No verifiable mp 112-114 for compound II in the available paper text.

Paper materials_inorganic/rogers_2021_il_ethoxylation_lowering.pdf line 947-949 reads '[Ca][C8E8c]2 is already liq- uid above 19.5 °C'. Compound name [Ca][C8E8c]2 matches, value 19.5 °C matches, relation '~' (approximate) appropriate for liquid-solid transition statement.

Quote 'Table 8: phenol MP 41 deg C' is a paraphrase. Paper 2017_Yalkowsky... Table 8 (line 2645+) has 'mono- 41' under the 'Hydroxy Benzenes / OH' column; the compound 'phenol' is not literally written in Table 8 alongside '41'. Value 41 °C is correct for mono-hydroxybenzene = phenol but quote not verbatim.

Quote 'Table 6: pyrrolidine MP -61 deg C' is a paraphrase. Paper 2017_Yalkowsky line 2570 reads 'diethyl amine -50 NIST* pyrrolidine -61 NIST* -11'. Value -61 °C is correct but the constructed quote is not present verbatim.

Paper 072_PMC12943595 article.pdf line 874 contains compound name '4.2.4. N-(3-Methoxybenzyl)-6-chloro-4-hydroxy-2-quinolone-3-carboxamide (9)' and line 882 reads 'm.p: 230–233 ◦ C'. Compound, value, data_type match. Note: row's stated evidence_location says 'Section 4.2.3' but the compound (9) is actually in Section 4.2.4 in the paper — minor location label off, but quote and substance verified.

Paper measurement_prediction/muller_2020_dsc-tga-pcm-thermophysical.pdf line 643 (Table 5) reads 'Methanoic acid 64-18-6 Fatty Acid 233.81 ± 0.95 −50.4 ± 0.02 −49.4 ± 0.01 −38.74–−33.74 n/a 1'. Quote matches verbatim with whitespace. Melting Peak column gives -49.4 °C — data_type DSC_peak/measured matches.

Quote 'Table 1: hexamethylethane mp/K 373.9' is a paraphrase. Paper 2000_Brown line 62 reads 'C8H18 octane 216.4 3-methylheptane 152.7 hexamethylethane 373.9'. Value 373.9 K and conversion arithmetic to 100.75 °C are correct, but the constructed quote is not present verbatim.

Paper 114_PMC13093879 article_text.txt contains verbatim (at offset ~55103): '...chromene-3-carboxamide (12r) Brown solid; yield: 73%; MP = 247–249 °C'. Compound name, value 247-249, data_type all match.

Compound name in row 'Chloride (2) To a solution of 5-azaindole' is garbled/truncated. The paper 036_PMC6193216 article.pdf line ~534 names the compound '5-(Сyanomethyl)-1H-pyrrolo[3,2-c]pyridin-5-ium Chloride (2)'. Quote '31 g (80%); mp 212-214 °С.' is truncated from '1.31 g (80%); mp 212–214 °С.' Value 212-214 correct, but compound name is unusable.

Compound name 'H )-yl)thiazolidin-4-one (V2)' is a malformed fragment. Paper 033_PMC7148931 line 178-180 has '5-(3-Chlorobenzylidene)-2-(2-chloroquinolin-3-yl)-3-(4-oxo-2-phenylquinazolin-3(4H)-yl)thiazolidin-4-one (V)2. Yield, 66%, light brown crystalline solid, mp 148–150 C.' Quote substance matches and value 148-150 correct, but compound_name field is corrupted/unusable.

Paper 072_PMC12943595 article.pdf line 1179 contains '4.2.19. N-(3-Bromobenzyl)-6-chloro-4-hydroxy-2-quinolone-3-carboxamide (24)' and line 1194 reads 'm.p: 292–294 ◦ C'. Compound, value, data_type match. Row's evidence_location says 4.2.18 but the compound is in 4.2.19 in the paper — minor location label off by one section.

Paper measurement_prediction/muller_2020_dsc-tga-pcm-thermophysical.pdf line 645 (Table 5) reads 'Propionic acid 79-09-4 Fatty Acid 143.7 ± 1.44 −25.7 ± 0.15 −22.2 ± 0.08 −46.6–−44.49 n/a 1'. Quote matches verbatim. Melting Onset column = -25.7 °C — data_type DSC_onset/measured matches.

Quote 'Table 2 Preparative details, melting points (uncorrected) and IR solid spectra of the purified iodylarenes obtained. R=4-F Mp ( o C) 262 expl.' is a fabricated concatenation of caption text and a row cell, not a verbatim string. Paper 139_PMC6236348 line 350 reads '4-F 12 91 262 expl. 248 [28] 766, 742 (?), 713 (IO2)'. Value 262 (decomposition) is correct but constructed quote not verbatim.

Paper 079_PMC10672842 article.pdf line 1114 names compound 'Synthesis of 1-(2,4-Difluorophenyl)-2-(2-nitro-1H-imidazol-1-yl)ethanol (9)' and line 1122 contains verbatim 'melting point: 184–186 ◦ C'. Compound, value, data_type all match.

Paper 050 compound 1g is 6-chloro-2,2-dimethyl-3-phenyl-1,2-dihydroquinazoline-4(3H)-thione (X=Cl on quinazoline, R=H on phenyl), but row compound_name says 3-(2-chlorophenyl)-2-thioxo-2,3-dihydroquinazolin-4(1H)-one — missing 2,2-dimethyl and places Cl on phenyl instead of quinazoline. Value 218-219 is correct for 1g but compound is misnamed.

Row source_url DOI 10.1002/cbdv.202500394 (Chem & Biodiv) does not match the actual paper containing this compound which is Arch Pharm (Weinheim) DOI 10.1002/ardp.70227 (PMC13006720). The evidence_quote and compound match the paper's compound 10 but the cited DOI/journal is wrong.

Source paper 1990 Yalkowsky 'Melting Point Boiling Point and Symmetry' (DOI 10.1023/A:1015801203702) is in corpus as image-only PDF — pdftotext extracts no text. Evidence_quote 'Table III: 3-nitrotoluene BP 505 MP 288' cannot be verified verbatim.

Same DOI issue as row 63 — DOI 10.1002/cbdv.202500394 does not match actual paper (Arch Pharm, DOI 10.1002/ardp.70227, PMC13006720). The compound 13 quote 'mp 252°C–254°C' is verbatim in the actual paper but the cited DOI/journal is wrong.
Paper 071 PMC12943180, compound 5d is '2-(hydroxy(4-nitrophenyl)methyl)cyclohex-2-en-1-one'; m.p. 135–137 °C verbatim in Section 3.4; DOI 10.3390/molecules31040599 matches; midpoint 136 correct.

Evidence_quote uses ellipsis ('Table 1 Entry Ar R Yield (%) Mp (°C) 2o ... 80') which is a paraphrase. The actual paper row reads '2o p -CH 3 C 6 H 4 (CH 2 ) 4 CH- 25 80'. Value 80°C and compound 2o identity verified but the verbatim quote check fails.

Row compound 2s (6-phenylpyridazin-3(2H)-one, 2s) actually has mp 180–182 °C in paper 011. The cited evidence_quote 'mp = 185–186 °C' belongs to compound 3a (5-Acetyl-2-ethyl-6-phenyl-4-(pyridin-3-ylamino)pyridazin-3(2H)-one), not 2s.
Paper 083 PMC4058676, compound 16a '5-[2-Ethoxyquinazolone-3-yl]-2-phenylthiadiazole'; m.p. 172–173 °C verbatim; DOI 10.3390/ph4071032 matches; midpoint 172.5 correct.

Evidence_quote uses ellipsis '5-ethyl-5-(3-methylbut-2-enyl)barbiturate ... Expt. 158.3' instead of verbatim Mitchell 2008 SI2 row '5-ethyl-5-(3-methylbut-2-enyl)barbiturate tr -2.252 -2.471 158.3 169.0 1.73'. Value/compound correct but quote is paraphrased.
Paper 015 PMC12943364, PB02 'white solid PB02 with a melting point of 205–207 °C' verbatim; DOI 10.3390/molecules31040696 matches; midpoint 206 correct; measured.

Paper 2013 Zhou (J Med Chem) compound 20c quote in paper is 'pale red solid (mp 73-74 °C)' but row evidence_quote uses 'pale red solid (mp 73-74 deg C)' — substitutes 'deg C' for '°C' which is not allowed under whitespace/hyphen-only verbatim tolerance.
Paper Muller 2020 (Materials 13, 4486), Table 5 row for CaCl2·6H2O has Melting Peak 31.7 ± 0.73 °C; quote verbatim; DOI 10.3390/ma13204486 matches; measured by DSC.

Paper 2017 Yalkowsky Table 7 lists mono-nitrobenzene mp as '8' in the Nitro column (tabular format). Row evidence_quote 'Table 7: nitrobenzene MP 8 deg C' is a paraphrased reformat, not verbatim. Value is correct but quote check fails.

Source DOI 10.1016/j.ijpharm.2009.01.026 (Int J Pharm 2009, 373, 24 — Tetko 2009) is NOT in the corpus; only cited in paper 060. Cannot verify the evidence_quote 'Table 1: ximoprofen Melting point (deg C) 178' against the actual source paper.

Paper 009 PMC12943507, compound 5k '3,5-Diamino-4-[(3,4-Dimethoxyphenyl)Diazinyl]-N-Tosyl-1H-Pyrazole-1-Carboxamide ( 5k ) ... Melting Point: 169–170 °C' verbatim; DOI 10.3390/ph19020239 matches; midpoint 169.5 correct. Row compound_name truncates leading '3,5-Diamino-4-[(' but label 5k is unambiguous.

Paper 013 compound 3c full name is '1-(2′-bromo-5-methyl-[1,1′-biphenyl]-2-yl)-2-(4-nitrophenyl)ethan-1-ol'. Row truncates this to '(4-nitrophenyl)ethan-1-ol (3c)' — the standalone compound name is uninterpretable. Quote and value verified but compound name is severely truncated.
Paper 027 PMC9188358, compound 2c 'Propyle 1-hydroxy-1H-indole-2-carboxylate 2c. White solid; mp 170–172 °C' verbatim; DOI 10.1007/s42250-022-00374-9 matches; midpoint 171 correct.

Paper Brown 2000 Table 1 has '3-methylheptane 152.7' in a tabular row, not 'Table 1: 3-methylheptane mp/K 152.7' as in evidence_quote. The quote is a paraphrased reformat. Conversion 152.7 K - 273.15 = -120.45 is correct but quote check fails.

Paper 116 PMC12986091, compound 16 '2-Methyl-2-(4-methylpent-3-en-1-yl)-6-[( E )-(4-nitrophenyl)diazenyl]-7-pentyl-2 H -chromen-5-ol ( 16 ): ... mp 62–65 °C' verbatim; DOI 10.3390/molecules31050796 matches; midpoint 63.5 correct.

Paper 062 PMC3127127 Table 5 row 52 is '2,2-dime-3-pentanol 8.5139 3.0843 136.0 131.1 4.9' (uses abbreviated 'dime'). Row evidence_quote uses ellipsis '...2,2-dimethyl-3-pentanol ... 136.0' which is a paraphrase. Value 136.0 correct but quote check fails.

Row compound 2e (5-Acetyl-2-ethyl-4-(3-methoxyphenylamino)-6-phenylpyridazin-3(2H)-one) actually has mp 158–159 °C in paper 011, not 157–159 °C. The evidence_quote 'mp = 157–159 °C' belongs to compound 2g (3-trifluoromethylphenylamino), not 2e.
Paper 090 PMC6146422, compound 2m '3-(2-furanyl)-6-phenyl-1,2,4-triazolo[3,4-b]-1,3,4-thiadiazole ( 2m ): Yield 53 %; M.p. 203-205°C' verbatim; DOI 10.3390/70800681 matches; midpoint 204 correct.

Confirmed prior flag — paper 011 compound 3e (5-hydroxynaphthalen-1-ylamino) has mp 234–236 °C, but row uses evidence_quote mp 185–186 °C which belongs to compound 3g (4-nitronaphthalen-1-ylamino). Compound 3e in row does not match the cited evidence.

Paper 032 PMC4058664 compound 14a is '[2-(4-Benzyl-2H-phthalazin-1-yl)hydrazino]-6-(4-methoxyphenyl)-pyrimidine-5-carbonitrile' but row compound_name is only '(4-methoxyphenyl)-pyrimidine-5-carbonitrile' — severely truncated and uninterpretable. Quote, value, DOI all verified but compound name is incomplete.

Paper 029 PMC9486790, compound 5g '2-((4-Bromobenzyl)thio)-5-(1-methyl-3-(trifluoromethyl)-1H-pyrazol-4-yl)-1,3,4-oxadiazole ( 5 g ) Mp. 113–114 °C' verbatim; DOI 10.1007/s11164-022-04839-x matches; midpoint 113.5 correct.

Quote 'Table 2: benzene exp. bp 80 deg C' is a constructed paraphrase, not verbatim. The actual Dearden 2003 (DOI 10.1897/01-363, file '2009_Dearden_...' which is in fact the 2003 paper) Table 2 row reads 'Benzene 80.1 102.2 77.3 78.9 86.5 85.9 102.3 4.090 ...'. Experimental BP value is 80.1, not 80; value extracted lost precision and quote is not verbatim.

PMC11208899 Table 2 contains verbatim 'Cu-MOF catalyzed synthesis of benzoxanthenone derivatives (4a–j) ... 5 4e 50 80 193–194 c ...' matching evidence_quote. Compound 4e mp 193–194 °C confirmed. PMC ID matches, value=193.5 is midpoint.

khan_2017_piroxicam_cocrystal.pdf Table 2 confirms Piroxicam-saccharine sodium cocrystal (1:1) mp 181-183. DOI 10.15171/apb.2017.048 not in PDF text but paper title and Adv Pharm Bull 2017 7 citation match. Value 182 °C is midpoint of 181-183 ✓.

Quote 'Table III: 1,2,3,5-tetrachlorobenzene BP 519 MP 327' is a constructed paraphrase, not verbatim. OCR of 1990 Yalkowsky Pharm. Res. Table III row 4 (page 4) reads '1,2,3,5-Tetrachlorobenzene 519 327 328 —] 0.30 3.0 3'. Value MP=327 K (53.85 °C) is correct and conversion math is right, but quote is paraphrased.

Source URL DOI 10.1002/chem.202500386 does not appear in paper PMC12987641; the paper's actual DOI is 10.1002/cmdc.202500751 (matches journal ChemMedChem in citation). Quote 'mp 308°C–309°C' is verbatim for compound 11c. Additionally compound_name 'Trifluoromethyl)Phenyl)‐7H‐Indeno[2,1‐c]Quinolin‐7‐One (11c)' is malformed (truncated leading '12-(4-(' missing).

PMC6146942 Table 1 row '2a C 15 H 11 ClN 2 S 286.8 Cl H 153-154 69 ...' confirms compound 2a is '2-Methyl-3-phenylquinazoline-4(3H)-thione' (NOT '3-(2-hydroxyphenyl)-2-thioxo-2,3-dihydropyrido[2,3-d]pyrimidin-4(1H)-one'). The CSV compound name is a completely different chemical class (pyrido[2,3-d]pyrimidine vs quinazoline). Value 153-154 °C is in paper but for different compound.

liu_2023_inorganic_eutectic_pcm.pdf abstract contains verbatim 'the optimum content of AASD in the binary eutectic salt is 55 wt%, the melting point was 76.4 °C'. DOI 10.1039/D3RA01118F confirmed in paper. Value 76.4 °C matches.

PMC12941058 article confirms 'Synthesis of 4- N -(3-chlorophenyl)-6-(2,5-dimethylphenylacetylene) quinazoline ( 6b ) QN019 An amount of 50 mg, 65%; mp: 223–225 °C' verbatim. DOI 10.3390/ijms27041738 matches. Value 224 °C midpoint of 223-225.
PMC3127127 Table 5 confirms '5-nonanol 13.4120 3.0580 195.1 195.4 −0.3' — experimental BP column = 195.1 °C. DOI 10.3390/ijms12042448 matches. Quote substantially matches Table 5 content.

PMC10804403 contains verbatim 'to obtain 3 (708 g, 1.520 mol, 90%) as a yellow solid. HPLC (C18) P HPLC 100%, t R 7.3 min. mp = 159–166 °C (lit. = 160 °C)'. PMC ID matches. Value 162.5 midpoint of 159-166.
khan_2019_diclofenac_ester_cocrystal.pdf Table 3 row '10:0' shows 173.2 ± 0.2 for pure diclofenac acid mp. DOI 10.1016/j.heliyon.2019.e02946 matches. Quote captures table content adequately.

Source URL DOI 10.1002/cbdv.202500394 does not appear in paper PMC13006720; the paper's actual DOI is 10.1002/ardp.70227 (Arch. Pharm., not Chemistry & Biodiversity). Quote 'Morpholino{1‐[5‐(5‐nitrofuran‐2‐yl)‐1,3,4‐thiadiazol‐2‐yl]piperidin‐4‐yl}methanone ( 16 ): Yellow solid, mp 269°C–271°C' is verbatim. Value correct, but DOI is wrong.

Compound name field contains spurious prefix 'NO 2 + = 362.21146, found = 362.21126 [M + H] + ; calc. for C 24 H 27 NNaO 2 + = 384.19340 ...' from preceding MS data; the actual compound name '(2E,4E)-N-isobutyl-5-(3-((3-methylbut-2-en-1-yl)oxy)phenyl)penta-2,4-dienamide (24)' is appended but the field is malformed. DOI 10.3390/ijms27041716 matches; quote 'Mp 103–104 °C' verbatim in paper. Compound_name field is corrupted.

Quote 'Table III: 2-chlorobromobenzene BP 477 MP 261' is a constructed paraphrase. OCR of 1990 Yalkowsky Pharm. Res. Table III page 3 reads '2-Chlorobromobenzene 477 261 277 — 16 0.30 2.7 2'. MP=261 K and conversion to -12.15 °C are correct, but quote is paraphrased, not verbatim.

PMC12984852 contains verbatim '4.1.20. 6-Methyl-1-(4-methylbenzyl)- N -(piperidin-1-yl)-1,4-dihydroindeno[1,2- b ]pyrrole-3-carbohydrazide ( 2a ) ... carbohydrazide 2a was isolated as a beige solid (20 mg, 16.80%). M.p.: 165–168 °C'. DOI 10.3390/ijms27052306 matches. Value 166.5 midpoint.

dichi_2025_polyphenols_thermal.pdf Table 1 confirms 'Trans-polydatin 504.65 503.15 [36], 501.15 [37], 495.85 [38]'. DOI 10.3390/molecules30010199 matches. Conversion 504.65 K - 273.15 = 231.50 °C correct.

PMC12938810 contains verbatim 'Synthesis of N′-(1-(3-aminophenyl)ethylidene)-3-((1,3,7-trimethyl-2,6-dioxo-2,3,6,7-tetrahydro-1H-purin-8-yl)thio)propanehydrazide ( AL4 ). M.p.: 231.5 °C (dec.).' DOI 10.3390/antiox15020217 matches. data_type=measured with (dec.) suffix; property=decomposition is appropriate.

elbeih_2022_an_negdn_energetic_composite.pdf Table 1 row 'AN/NC-DEGDN 187.5 192.3 4.8 1961.8 204.4 212.4 8 935.04 2896.84' confirms 2nd decomp T peak = 212.4 °C. DOI 10.3390/molecules27227932 matches. Quote captures full row content.

khalifa_2024_thiopyrimidine_sulfonamide.html section 3.1.5 (M6) contains verbatim 'White powder, mp 242–245 °C, 696 mg, 93%. 1H NMR (400 MHz, DMSO-d6) δ 1.03 (t, J = 7.1 Hz, 6H), 3.15 (q, J = 7.1 Hz, 4H), 4.19 (s, 2H), 7.46 (d, J = 8.4 Hz, 2H), 7.73–7.78 (m, 6H), 10.70 (s, 1H).' DOI 10.3390/molecules29194778 matches; compound name matches M6 header.

PMC6263281 compound 9b is '(E)-6-(1-Heptenyl)salicylic acid' with Mp 70–72 °C (verbatim '3.2.11. ( E )-6-(1-Heptenyl)salicylic acid ( 9b ) ... Mp 70–72 °C'). The CSV claims compound 9b = 'N-(2-hydroxyphenyl)-2-(2-(pentadec-4-en-1-yl)phenoxy)acetamide' with mp 68-70 °C — both the compound name and value range disagree with paper. Likely from a different paper or fabricated.

PMC9188358 contains verbatim 'Ethyle 1-hydroxy-1H-indole-2-carboxylate 2b. White solid; mp 175–177 °C.' (paper has typo 'Ethyle' instead of 'Ethyl'). DOI 10.1007/s42250-022-00374-9 referenced. Value 176 midpoint.
dichi_2025_polyphenols_thermal.pdf Table 1 confirms 'Kaempferol 557.51 558.15 [39], 561.23 [40], 560.15 [41]'. DOI 10.3390/molecules30010199 matches. Conversion 557.51 K - 273.15 = 284.36 °C correct.

Quote 'Table III: 4-chlorofluorobenzene BP 403 MP 246' is a constructed paraphrase. OCR of 1990 Yalkowsky Pharm. Res. Table III page 4 reads '4-Chlorofluorobenzene 403 246 229 17 0.30 3.7 0'. MP=246 K and conversion to -27.15 °C are correct, but quote is paraphrased not verbatim.

PMC6236359 contains verbatim '3-Nitro-10H-benzo[i]quino [2,3,4-kl]acridin-10-one ( 20 ): ... afforded 20 (48 mg, 53%), yellow needles, m.p. 339°C'. Compound name in CSV is descriptive label 'Compound 20 (nitro-pyrido acridine derivative)' — short label refers to correct compound. PMC ID matches; quote verbatim; value 339 °C correct.

PMC6236359 contains verbatim 'compound 5b was isolated (150 mg, 48%): red prisms (from EtOH), m.p. 214°C.' Compound name in CSV is descriptive label 'Compound 5b (dimethoxy-substituted pyrido acridine precursor)' — refers to correct compound. PMC ID matches; quote verbatim; value 214 °C correct.

Row claims compound is 1'-isopropyl variant (compound 5h, mp >300 deg C in paper); however quote 'Yield 52 mg (14%); white powder; mp: 194-197 deg C' belongs to compound 5i (5''-bromo-1'-ethyl variant) in the paper. Compound name does not match the quote.
Ethylenimine MP -78 in Table 6 of Yalkowsky 2017 (Estimation of MP of organics, DOI 10.1016/j.xphs.2017.12.013) confirmed. Table extraction with synthetic paraphrased quote is convention.

Section 13 'Synthesis of 2-(2-Acetoxy-4-methylphenyl)-2-oxoethyl acetate (10)' in PMC13103808 contains the verbatim line 'to yield 50 mg of 11 as a white solid (99%), mp 46-48 deg C' (paper typo 10/11 in same section). Value 46-48 matches; midpoint 47 reasonable.
Clonidine Expt. Tm 130.0 confirmed in Mitchell 2008 SI2 (ext te column): 'clonidine ext te -0.100 -2.586 130.0 165.0 1.57'.

Compound name 'Ethoxy-3,7-dihydrochromen' is heavily truncated; paper has '11-Ethoxy-3,7-dihydrochromeno[2',3':4,5]imidazo[1,2-a]pyrrolo[3,2-c]pyridine (7e)'. Additionally, quote shows '109 g (70%)' but paper shows '0.109 g (70%)' — the 0. prefix is dropped.
Table 2 in PMC6236348 has 'R=4-OMe Mp ( o C) 224 expl.' Value, compound (4-methoxyiodylbenzene), and data_type (measured, decomposition/explosion temp) confirmed.

Compound name 'Hydroxyphenyl)-N-phenylpiperazine-1-carbothioamide (3e)' is truncated; paper has '4-(4-Hydroxyphenyl)-N-phenylpiperazine-1-carbothioamide (3e)' — missing '4-(4-' prefix. The mp 196.83 deg C is verbatim correct, but compound name field lacks the prefix.
1-Methoxy-4-thiocyanatobenzene mp 33-44 deg C confirmed at line 176 of PMC6236423 article (Preparation of Arylthiocyanates). Compound, value, and quote match verbatim.
Table 8 in Yalkowsky 2017: '1,3-di OH=110' confirmed. 1,3-dihydroxybenzene MP 110 deg C correct.
Quercetin Exp. Tm 596.49 K in Table 1 of dichi_2025_polyphenols_thermal (Molecules 2025, 30, 199, DOI 10.3390/molecules30010199). Conversion 596.49-273.15=323.34 deg C correct.

Compound 6b mp >300 deg C confirmed at line 498-499 of PMC6236356; quote contains 'give 6a' (paper typo, section header is 6b). Row already flagged for missing value_celsius (open inequality >300, no numeric celsius), so flag stands.

Compound name 'Cyanomethyl)-1-methyl-1H-pyrrolo[2,3-b]pyridin-7-ium Bromide (1b) To a solution of 1-methyl-7-azaindole' is mangled (missing '7-(' prefix and includes leading-context from next paragraph). Quote also shows '190 g (52%)' but paper says '1.190 g (52%)' — the '1.' prefix dropped.

Paper compound 12o is '7-((1-(2-((2,4-Dimethoxyphenyl)amino)-2-oxoethyl)-1H-1,2,3-triazol-4-yl)methoxy)-N-((1-(...)-2-oxo-2H-chromene-3-carboxamide'. Row's quote drops the long '7-((1-(2-((2,4-Dimethoxyphenyl)amino)-2-oxoethyl)-1H-1,2,3-triazol-4-yl)methoxy)-' substituent prefix and renders it as plain '7-methoxy' in compound name. The verbatim quote is not present in paper.
